quickFacts: Birthplace of paella — never order it with chorizo here
The old Turia riverbed is now a 9km urban park and bike path
Spain's third-largest city but way more affordable than Barcelona or Madrid
Las Fallas festival in March is one of Europe's wildest

places.name: City of Arts and Sciences
places.caption: Futuristic architectural marvel
places.description: Santiago Calatrava's sci-fi complex has an aquarium, planetarium, opera house, and science museum. The reflecting pools at sunset are iconic.

places.name: Turia Gardens
places.caption: 9km park in a former riverbed
places.description: Rent a bike and cruise through this green ribbon of gardens, playgrounds, and sports facilities that winds through the city. Free Gulliver playground is a blast.

places.name: Barrio del Carmen
places.caption: Historic old town with street art
places.description: Medieval streets meet colourful murals, indie boutiques, and tapas bars. Wander without a plan and discover hidden plazas and rooftop terraces.

places.name: Mercado Central
places.caption: Stunning Art Nouveau market hall
places.description: One of Europe's largest covered markets. Browse 1,000+ stalls of fresh seafood, cured meats, and local produce. Grab horchata and fartons for a snack.

places.name: Playa de la Malvarrosa
places.caption: City beach with seafood restaurants
places.description: Wide sandy beach just a bus ride from the centre. The beachfront promenade is lined with paella restaurants — this is where locals actually eat it.

places.name: La Lonja de la Seda
places.caption: UNESCO Gothic silk exchange
places.description: A masterpiece of late Gothic architecture with twisting columns that look like palm trees. One of the most beautiful buildings in Spain and free on Sundays.

places.name: Albufera Natural Park
places.caption: Lagoon, rice paddies & sunset boat rides
places.description: Just 10km south, this freshwater lagoon is where paella was born. Take a boat ride at sunset through the rice fields — genuinely stunning.

places.name: Ruzafa
places.caption: Trendy neighbourhood for brunch & nightlife
places.description: Valencia's hipster quarter with the best brunch spots, craft beer bars, vintage shops, and a buzzing weekend nightlife scene.

places.name: Torres de Serranos
places.caption: Medieval city gate with rooftop views
places.description: Climb the 14th-century towers for panoramic views across the old town and the Turia gardens. Entry is free on Sundays.

places.name: Oceanogràfic
places.caption: Europe's largest aquarium
places.description: Part of the City of Arts complex, this massive aquarium has beluga whales, sharks, and a walk-through underwater tunnel. Worth the ticket price.

places.name: El Cabanyal
places.caption: Fishing quarter
places.description: Colourful tile-fronted houses in the former fishermen's district. Now hip with restaurants, street art and a weekly market.

places.name: Bioparc Valencia
places.caption: Immersive zoo
places.description: African-themed zoo without visible barriers between you and the animals. Gorillas, elephants, lemurs in naturalistic habitats.

foodScene: Paella valenciana is the real deal here — made with rabbit, chicken, and beans, never seafood (that's a separate dish). Horchata de chufa is the local tiger-nut drink served ice-cold with fartons (dunking pastries). Agua de Valencia is the local cocktail — cava, OJ, vodka, and gin. Eat lunch at 2pm like a local.
